footer.description: A sanctuary of peace and a site of the miraculous Eucharistic event. We welcome pilgrims and visitors from around the world to experience the divine presence in the heart of Kerala.

hero.title:
Christ the King
Church
hero.subtitle: A sanctuary of faith nestled in the hills of Kerala, where history and divine grace intertwine.

history.title:
A Legacy of Faith
in Malabar
history.p1: The history of Christ the King Church is deeply rooted in the migration of farming communities to the hilly terrains of Malabar in the mid-20th century. Seeking fertile land and a new life, these pioneers brought with them a profound Catholic faith.
history.p2: Initially, spiritual needs were met through makeshift chapels and visiting priests. As the community grew, the need for a permanent place of worship became evident. Through immense sacrifice, collective effort, and unwavering devotion, the first church structure was erected, becoming the spiritual heartbeat of Vilakkannur.

location.title:
Nestled in the
Hills of Kerala
location.p1: Vilakkannur is a serene, rural village characterized by its lush green landscapes, rolling hills, and tranquil atmosphere. Far from the noise of the city, the church's location naturally fosters a sense of peace and contemplation.
location.p2: The architecture of the church blends traditional Kerala elements with classic Catholic design, creating a space that feels both majestic and deeply welcoming. The surrounding nature acts as a silent witness to the prayers of thousands who visit seeking solace.

growth.title: A Global Pilgrimage Center
growth.subtitle: What was once a quiet parish serving local farmers has blossomed into an international beacon of faith, drawing believers from every corner of the globe.

community.title: Community Growth
community.desc: Following the Eucharistic Miracle of 2013 and its subsequent Vatican recognition in 2025, the church experienced unprecedented growth. The local community expanded its mission to welcome, house, and guide the massive influx of pilgrims, transforming the village economy and social fabric while maintaining its spiritual core.

spiritual.title: Spiritual Importance
spiritual.desc: Today, Christ the King Church stands as a profound testament to the Real Presence. It is a place of deep healing, where thousands report experiencing spiritual renewal, physical healings, and a profound sense of peace. The sanctuary serves as a living reminder that the divine actively touches the human experience.

hero.title:
The Eucharistic
Miracle
hero.subtitle: A profound manifestation of Christ's real presence that drew the world's eyes to Vilakkannur.

01.title: The Moment
01.subtitle: November 15, 2013
01.desc: A quiet morning Mass. As the priest elevated the host, an extraordinary light emerged, altering the course of a small parish forever.

02.title: The Appearance
02.subtitle: The Face of Christ
02.desc: The distinct face of Jesus Christ, crowned with thorns, formed on the consecrated bread. A congregation was left in absolute awe and silence.

03.title: The Investigation
03.subtitle: Science & Theology
03.desc: Years of rigorous scrutiny. Independent laboratories and the Vatican's careful examination of the inexplicable, confirming the absence of artificial pigments.

04.title: The Recognition
04.subtitle: Vatican Approval, 2025
04.desc: Officially recognized as a true Eucharistic Miracle. A profound gift to the universal Church and a testament to divine presence in the modern age.

05.title: The Impact
05.subtitle: A Global Sanctuary
05.desc: Thousands journey to Vilakkannur seeking solace, healing, and a deeper connection with the Divine. A quiet village transformed into a beacon of faith.

hero.title: Visit Vilakkannur
hero.subtitle: Essential information for pilgrims and travelers seeking to experience the sanctuary.

bestTime.title: Best Time to Visit
bestTime.desc: The ideal time to visit is between September and March when the weather in Kerala is pleasant and cool.

tips.title: Tips for Pilgrims
tips.01: Maintain silence and decorum within the church premises.
tips.02: Modest attire is requested for all visitors entering the sanctuary.
tips.03: Photography inside the church during Holy Mass is strictly prohibited.
tips.04: For large group pilgrimages, please contact the parish office in advance.

air.title: By Air
air.desc: Kannur International Airport (CNN) is the nearest airport, located approximately 45 km away. Taxis are readily available.

train.title: By Train
train.desc: Kannur Railway Station (CAN) is the major railhead, about 50 km from the church.

road.title: By Road
road.desc: Regular bus services operate from Kannur and Taliparamba. The route is via Taliparamba - Sreekandapuram - Naduvil.

01.q: Is there an entry fee to visit the church?
01.a: No, there is no entry fee. The church is open to all visitors and pilgrims free of charge.

02.a: While the village itself has limited commercial lodging, there are several guesthouses and hotels in nearby towns like Taliparamba and Kannur.

03.q: Can I see the miraculous host?
03.a: Yes, the miraculous host is installed for public veneration at the church. However, viewing times may be subject to the parish schedule.

04.a: Yes, the main sanctuary and primary facilities are accessible to visitors with mobility needs.

directions.title: Quick Directions
directions.desc: Located 45km from Kannur International Airport. Take the Taliparamba - Sreekandapuram route towards Naduvil. The church is prominently visible from the main road.
